text:
Market today: Mixed and nervous. The S&P flagging a potential breakdown is the real driver — Fed's Goolsbee saying inflation is still too high kills rate-cut hopes, and that's a headwind for high-multiple tech and chip names exactly like yours. Microsoft's historic June selloff signals big-cap tech is under real pressure.

Your holdings: Three need attention. PLTR is your biggest problem — down 17.56% and on a SELL signal; on margin, that hole gets deeper fast. AVGO is your largest position, down 13.18% with a TRIM flag — size alone makes this dangerous if it keeps sliding. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no trading history; it's only 0.65% in the green but one bad session could flip that hard. Margin amplifies every one of these drops.

Watch: Whether SPY breaks its critical level — a confirmed move lower drags your whole book. Also watch if the Microsoft selloff spreads to other big-cap tech names in your universe.

Bottom line: Your margin exposure plus three flagged positions in a potentially breaking market is the only thing that matters right now.
